subject: SCIENCE
title: Heat and Electricity
objective: Identify safe everyday sources and uses through on-screen sorting only.
introduction: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Heat and Electricity”.
A key word used in the lesson “Heat and Electricity”.
A key word used in the lesson “Heat and Electricity”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: What should a child do about a damaged cord?

options: Tell a responsible adult and do not touch it.

feedback.correct: Correct: Tell a responsible adult and do not touch it.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. Repair it while plugged in. and Put it in water. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Heat and Electricity”?

options.text: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

The correct response is “sunlight can warm surfaces” because it follows the lesson explanation: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Insert object in outlet” because it follows the lesson explanation: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

items.prompt: What should a child do about a damaged cord?

options.text: Tell a responsible adult and do not touch it.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Tell a responsible adult and do not touch it.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “adult handles damaged cord” because it follows the lesson explanation: Heat is energy transfer related to temperature differences; electricity involves electric charge. Do not touch hot objects, outlets, damaged cords, or loose batteries.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Heat and Electricity; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Heat and Electricity.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
